page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.16xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.hfg6 family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 64 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The KVM-hypervised architecture ensures dedicated CPU allocation, which minimizes resource contention and provides predictable compute performance for workloads requiring sustained CPU execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated physical cores offer a balanced resource profile for general-purpose and compute-heavy workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for deployments that do not require hardware acceleration or local disk space. This server is suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, and general-purpose compute workloads requiring high-frequency dedicated cores.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.16xlarge is an x86_64 server instance in the ecs.hfg6 family, powered by a KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 3.1 GHz with a single thread per core, providing 64 vCPUs. The instance includes 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It does not contain local storage or GPUs, relying on external storage solutions, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource sharing. This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and compute-intensive tasks that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated physical execution units.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core x86_64 compute platform with a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and 20 Gbps baseline network performance.
